items.name: Async FSSpec
items.description: The Flyte async fsspec plugin is a powerful addition to the Flyte ecosystem designed to optimize the performance of object transmission. This plugin focuses on overriding key methods of the file systems in fsspec to introduce efficiency improvements, resulting in accelerated data transfers between Flyte workflows and object storage.

items.name: AWS Athena
items.description: Flyte backend can be connected with Athena. Once enabled, it allows you to query AWS Athena service (Presto + ANSI SQL Support) and retrieve typed schema (optionally).

items.name: AWS Batch
items.description: Flyte backend can be connected with AWS batch. Once enabled, it allows you to run flyte task on AWS batch service

items.name: AWS SageMaker
items.description: The plugin currently features a SageMaker deployment connector.

items.name: Comet ML
items.description: Comet’s machine learning platform integrates with your existing infrastructure and tools so you can manage, visualize, and optimize models—from training runs to production monitoring. This plugin integrates Flyte with Comet.ml by configuring links between the two platforms.

items.name: DBT
items.description: Flytekit plugin for performing DBT tasks. Currently it supports  dbt run , dbt test, dbt source freshness tasks.

items.name: Deck
items.description: This plugin provides additional renderers to improve task visibility within Flytekit.

items.name: Dgxc-lepton
items.description: A professional Flytekit plugin that enables seamless deployment and management of AI inference endpoints using Lepton AI infrastructure within Flyte workflows.

items.name: Dolt
items.description: The DoltTable plugin is a wrapper that uses Dolt to move data between pandas.DataFrame’s at execution time and database tables at rest.

items.name: DuckDB
items.description: Run analytical workloads with ease using DuckDB.

items.name: Envd
items.description: envd is a command-line tool that helps you create the container-based development environment for AI/ML.

items.name: Flyte Interactive
items.description: FlyteInteractive plugin provides users' favorite interface to develop and debug a flyte task interactively. We support vscode, jupyter (WIP), and neovim (WIP).

items.name: FSSpec
items.description: This plugin provides an implementation of the data persistence layer in Flytekit that uses fsspec. Once this plugin

items.name: Geopandas
items.description: GeoPandas GeoPandas is an open source project to make working with geospatial data in python easier.

items.name: Google IAP
items.description: GCP Identity Aware Proxy (IAP) is a managed Google Cloud Platform (GCP) service that makes it easy to protect applications deployed on GCP by verifying user identity and using context to determine whether a user should be granted access. Because requests to applications protected with IAP first have to pass IAP before they can reach the protected backends, IAP provides a convenient way to implement a zero-trust access model.

items.name: Great Expectations
items.description: Great Expectations helps enforce data quality. The plugin supports the usage of Great Expectations as task and type.

items.name: Hive
items.description: Flyte backend can be connected with various Hive services. Once enabled, it allows you to query a Hive service (e.g., Qubole) and retrieve typed schema (optionally).

items.name: Hugging Face
items.description: Hugging Face is a community and data science platform that provides: Tools that enable users to build, train and deploy ML models based on open source (OS) code and technologies

items.name: Inference
items.description: Serve models natively in Flyte tasks using inference providers like NIM, Ollama, and others.

items.name: Kubeflow MPI
items.description: This plugin uses the Kubeflow MPI Operator and provides an extremely simplified interface for executing distributed training.

items.name: Kubeflow PyTorch
items.description: This plugin uses the Kubeflow PyTorch Operator and provides an extremely simplified interface for executing distributed training using various PyTorch backends.

items.name: Kubeflow TensorFlow
items.description: This plugin uses the Kubeflow TensorFlow Operator and provides an extremely simplified interface for executing distributed training using various TensorFlow backends.

items.name: Kubernetes Pod
items.description: By default, Flyte tasks decorated with @task are essentially single functions that are loaded in one container. But often, there is a need to run a job with more than one container.

items.name: Kubernetes Stateful
items.description: This plugin provides support for Kubernetes StatefulSet and Service integration, enabling seamless provisioning and coordination with any Kubernetes services or Flyte tasks. It is especially suited for deep learning use cases at scale, where distributed and parallelized data loading and caching across nodes are required.

items.name: Memory Machine Cloud
items.description: Flyte Connector plugin to allow executing Flyte tasks using MemVerge Memory Machine Cloud.

items.name: Memray Profiling
items.description: Memray tracks and reports memory allocations, both in python code and in compiled extension modules.

items.name: Modin
items.description: Modin is a pandas-accelerator that helps handle large datasets. It is a light-weight extension that is similar to the pandas API. It uses the concept of parallelism to reduce overhead, and improve the performance of pandas operations by leveraging the compute resources available.

items.name: Neptune
items.description: Neptune is the MLOps stack component for experiment tracking. It offers a single place to log, compare, store, and collaborate on experiments and models. This plugin integrates Flyte with Neptune by configuring links between the two platforms.

items.name: ONNX PyTorch
items.description: This plugin allows you to generate ONNX models from your PyTorch models.

items.name: ONNX ScikitLearn
items.description: This plugin allows you to generate ONNX models from your ScikitLearn models.

items.name: ONNX TensorFlow
items.description: This plugin allows you to generate ONNX models from your TensorFlow Keras models.

items.name: Optuna (wrapper)
items.description: This documentation provides a guide to a fully parallelized Flyte plugin for Optuna. This wrapper leverages Flyte's scalable and distributed workflow orchestration capabilities to parallelize Optuna's hyperparameter optimization across multiple trials efficiently.

items.name: Perian Job Platform
items.description: Flyte Connector plugin for executing Flyte tasks on Perian Job Platform (perian.io).

items.name: Slurm
items.description: The Slurm connector is designed to integrate Flyte workflows with Slurm-managed high-performance computing (HPC) clusters, enabling users to leverage Slurm's capability of compute resource allocation, scheduling, and monitoring.

items.name: SQLAlchemy
items.description: SQLAlchemy is the Python SQL toolkit and Object Relational Mapper that gives application developers the full power and flexibility of SQL. Flyte provides an easy-to-use interface to utilize SQLAlchemy to connect to various SQL Databases.

items.name: Vaex
items.description: Vaex is a high-performance Python library for lazy out-of-core DataFrames

items.name: whylogs
items.description: whylogs is an open source library for logging any kind of data. With whylogs,
